description: OPERATING_ROOM can only perform one surgery at a time
CT_SCANNER can only serve one patient at a time
MRI can only serve one patient at a time
ICU_BEDS are a limited shared capacity (Counter, initial=2)
VENTILATOR can only be used by one patient at a time
ANESTHESIOLOGIST must confirm readiness before surgery can begin
All medication prescriptions must pass PHARMACIST review
Surgical request from ER_DOCTOR_A/ER_DOCTOR_B to SURGEON coordinates operative care
ICU admission requests from ER_DOCTOR_A/ER_DOCTOR_B to ICU_COORDINATOR
ER_DOCTOR_A decides trauma patient treatment plan (surgery vs conservative)
ER_DOCTOR_B decides stroke patient needs (imaging type, ICU admission)
Anesthesia readiness must precede surgery start
Imaging results must be available before surgical or ICU decisions
Both ER_DOCTOR_A and ER_DOCTOR_B may need CT_SCANNER, MRI, and VENTILATOR for their respective patients
ICU_COORDINATOR manages bed allocation; both patients may need ICU_BEDS
Resource contention: both patients need OPERATING_ROOM, CT_SCANNER, and MRI simultaneously; agents must coordinate access to prevent circular wait
ICU_BEDS exhaustion: both patients need ICU admission but only 2 beds available; counter reaches 0 blocking further admissions
